author: Conradus Brunus
title: De caeremoniis libri sex
remarks: Dedication offers religious anthropology. Book 2, chapter 1: On the mysteries of the Mass. Cites Clichtove. Largely concerned to defend the historicity and apostolicity of the rites: relies heavily on Dionysius, the dogma of the Mass as sacrifice, prayer for the dead. For the rest, they are 'non sine magnorum mysteriorum significatione," but he refers us to other books on the subject. Counter-reform text. Cites Innocent III a few times but no one else.
